event_catalog.trigger_description: This event gets fired anytime a user either spends or earns virtual currency.
event_catalog.argument_description: <The amount of virtual currency being transacted. Negative if it's a spend and Positive if it's earned>
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub, Sizzle Sort Analytics
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: balance, currency, game_round_id, item, level, level_id, move_count, source
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include for every earn/spend of virtual currency; use signed amount convention.

event_catalog.trigger_description: This event will be fired when a limited-time event finishes (whether the user manages to complete it or the event is ended)
event_catalog.argument_description: <The event variant ID, of the event that has started>
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: config_ref, event_completed, event_count, game_round_id, level, levels_completed, mission_completed, mission_objective, position, source, stage, status
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include when limited-time event participation ends or expires.

event_catalog.trigger_description: This event will be fired when a progress was made and needs to be recorded in a limited time event
event_catalog.argument_description: <The event variant ID, of the event that has started>
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: config_ref, event_count, game_round_id, level, mission_completed, mission_objective, source, stage
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include when limited-time event progress milestones should be tracked.

event_catalog.trigger_description: This event will be fired every time a new limited time event has started for a user.
event_catalog.argument_description: <The event variant ID, of the event that has started>
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: config_ref, event_count, game_round_id, level, source, stage
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include for limited-time event participation start.

event_catalog.trigger_description: This event gets fired anytime a user finishes a game
event_catalog.argument_description: <the reason for the game round to end>
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub, Sizzle Sort Analytics
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: chapter_set_id, difficulty, extra_grill_used, game_count, game_end_reason, game_length, game_round_id, illegal_regions, layout_hash, level, level_bank_id, level_id, match_done, move_count, playon_delivery_failed_used, playon_out_of_time_used, playons_used, possible_matches, powerup_hint_used, powerup_hourglass_used, powerup_shuffle_used, powerup_takeaway_used, powerup_toolkit_used, powerup_used, powerup_{name}_used, revives_delivery_failed_used, revives_out_of_time_used, revives_used, rv_powerup_used, skip_delivery_used, time_limit, time_per_match, worms_target
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include when a round can complete, fail, be abandoned, or otherwise end.

event_catalog.trigger_description: This event gets fired anytime a user starts a new game round.
event_catalog.argument_description: <the state of game that has been started>
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub, Sizzle Sort Analytics
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: chapter_set_id, difficulty, game_count, game_round_id, level, level_bank_id, level_id, possible_matches
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include when the game has discrete rounds, levels, matches, or attempts.

event_catalog.trigger_description: This event gets fired anytime a user either spends or earns an item
event_catalog.argument_description: <The amount of item beings transacted. Negative if it's a spend and Positive if it's earned>
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub, Sizzle Sort Analytics
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: balance, game_round_id, item, level, level_id, move_count, source
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include for every earn/spend of boosters, powerups, lives, or other inventory items.

event_catalog.trigger_description: Triggered when the users open the Shop page
event_catalog.argument_description: Comma-separated list of UUIDs for the products that the user is seeing in the store, in the order that they appear in the store
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub, Sizzle Sort Analytics
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include when an in-game store or offer list can be opened.

event_catalog.trigger_description: Triggered when the purchase funnel for the product has failed for any reason.
event_catalog.argument_description: Product ID / Shops Catalog Product ID of the product that the user attempts to purchase
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub, Sizzle Sort Analytics
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: app_store_id, balance, config_ref, dollar_value, game_round_id, item_type, level, level_id, reason, source, transaction_id
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include when an IAP purchase attempt fails or is cancelled.

event_catalog.trigger_description: Triggered when the users click on the buy button of the product
event_catalog.argument_description: Product ID / Shops Catalog Product ID of the product that the user attempts to purchase
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub, Sizzle Sort Analytics
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: app_store_id, balance, config_ref, dollar_value, game_round_id, item_type, level, level_id, source
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include when the user initiates an IAP purchase.

event_catalog.trigger_description: Triggered when the user has successfully completed the purchase of the product
event_catalog.argument_description: Product ID / Shops Catalog Product ID of the product that the user attempts to purchase
event_catalog.source_coverage: Dot Paint Analytics, General Events Hub, Sizzle Sort Analytics
event_catalog.canonical_payload_fields: app_store_id, balance, config_ref, dollar_value, game_round_id, item_type, level, level_id, source, transaction_id
event_catalog.generator_guidance: Include when the app store confirms a completed IAP purchase.

generation_packs.applicable_when: Games with levels, rounds, attempts, win/lose/discontinue endings, or retry behavior.
generation_packs.notes: Sizzle and Dot Paint add genre-specific payloads such as possible_matches, move_count, time_limit, layout_hash, worms_target, and illegal_regions.

generation_packs.applicable_when: Games with soft currency, inventory, boosters, lives, powerups, or purchasable items.
Games with time-limited events, seasons, missions, milestones, or event-specific progress.
Games using platform-triggered ad events where extra payloads such as placement, level, and level_id must be supplied consistently.
generation_packs.recommended_events_or_platform_events: Ad_Call_Interstitial, Ad_Call_Rewarded, Ad_Click_Interstitial, Ad_Click_Rewarded, Ad_Close_Interstitial, Ad_Close_Rewarded, Ad_Impression_Interstitial, Ad_Impression_Rewarded
generation_packs.notes: Do not specify these as manually implemented events; ensure platform events receive the listed extra payloads.

generation_packs.applicable_when: Games where rewarded videos grant boosters, revives, play-ons, extra slots, skips, or currency effects.
generation_packs.launch_priority: Launch critical if rewarded ads exist
generation_packs.notes: Use transactions to track rewards/costs from rewarded ads; platform ad events are handled separately.

generation_packs.applicable_when: Games with an in-app store, product catalog, paid bundles, coins, ad removal, or purchase flow.

governance_decisions.decision: Use source and item as canonical fields for new specs.
governance_decisions.implementation_note: Keep aliases only for interpreting historical specs or existing pipelines.

governance_decisions.decision: Use dollar_value as the canonical field for new specs.
governance_decisions.implementation_note: Existing source workbooks still show dollar_vallue; generated specs should output dollar_value.

governance_decisions.decision: Do not create manual ad lifecycle event specs when platform events are automatically tracked.
governance_decisions.implementation_note: Use Platform Ad Payload Enrichment to require extra payloads on platform events.

review_questions.question: Should fields like possible_matches, time_per_match, extra_grill_used, skip_delivery_used, layout_hash, worms_target, and illegal_regions be split into genre/feature packs?
review_questions.why_it_matters: They are valuable for specific games but may not apply to every game using Game_Start/Game_End.
review_questions.recommendation: Keep them as feature/genre packs layered on top of the global Game_Start/Game_End events.

review_questions.question: Which payloads are truly required at ingestion time versus recommended for analysis?
review_questions.why_it_matters: The source files mostly imply requiredness through descriptions; the library currently marks it as inferred.
review_questions.recommendation: Add explicit required/optional governance in the next revision.
